question: Is the running boy from the first image composited onto the glacier landscape from the second image?
Is the boy positioned in the foreground of the glacier scene?
Is the glacier background (ice, mountains, and dark terrain) clearly visible and preserved?
Does the boy retain the specific running posture (mid-stride, arms bent) seen in the source image?
Is the boy wearing a blue t-shirt and tan/khaki shorts consistent with the input image?
Has the visual quality (sharpness and texture detail) of the boy been enhanced to match the high resolution of the background image?
Has the color temperature of the boy been adjusted to match the cold, bluish tones of the glacier (removing the warm/green cast from the original photo)?
Does the lighting on the boy appear consistent with the overcast, diffused lighting of the glacier scene?
Is the scale of the boy plausible relative to the foreground terrain (appearing as a small figure in a vast environment rather than a giant)?
Is the boy placed on the ground with appropriate contact shadows, avoiding a 'floating' appearance?
Are the edges of the composited subject blended naturally without jagged pixelation, halos, or residual green grass from the original image?
Is the boy's face and body anatomy rendered correctly without severe distortion or missing limbs?
